items.title: Interesting ratings item whether you follow NASCAR or not: Coke 600 ratings & viewership on Prime Video were considerably different on a "Big Data + Panel" basis than on the panel-only metric NASCAR is now publicizing.
items.author: Sports Media Watch
items.body_excerpt:
Interesting ratings item whether you follow NASCAR or not: Coke 600 ratings & viewership on Prime Video were considerably different on a "Big Data + Panel" basis than on the panel-only metric NASCAR is now publicizing.

Numbers tell two different stories: www.sportsmediawatch.com/2026/05/nasc...

media.title: NASCAR Coke 600 ratings tell different stories depending on methodology
media.description: NASCAR Coke 600 ratings tell two different stories depending on whether one uses Nielsen's "Big Data + Panel" or 'panel-only' methodologies.

items.title: In the before times, the Bucks would announce workouts and make some of the guys available to talk. Fans would get excited and it was good team PR.
items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
In the before times, the Bucks would announce workouts and make some of the guys available to talk. Fans would get excited and it was good team PR.

Since the team appears to be entering the after times, might want to bring that back.

items.title: In the before times, the Bucks used to bring in a ton of locals for a workout. Honestly, it was a fun day. The guys knew they weren't going to get drafted/signed. But part of a dream was realized & great team PR.
items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
In the before times, the Bucks used to bring in a ton of locals for a workout. Honestly, it was a fun day. The guys knew they weren't going to get drafted/signed. But part of a dream was realized & great team PR.

Since the team appears to be entering the after times, might want to bring that back.

items.title: Federal prosecutors filed new charges against NBA veteran Terry Rozier today. Prosecutors say Rozier took a roughly $100,000 "bribe" to exit early from a March 2023 Hornets game so a group of sports gamblers could win bets on his props.
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
Federal prosecutors filed new charges against NBA veteran Terry Rozier today. Prosecutors say Rozier took a roughly $100,000 "bribe" to exit early from a March 2023 Hornets game so a group of sports gamblers could win bets on his props.

The game in question...

(via Mike Vorkunov)
#NBA

items.title: A lawyer for Rozier, Jim Trusty, said: “The new indictment confirms that our motion to dismiss was a good one — it’s just new charges and new theories trotted out in the hope that something sticks.” Rozier has been charged with sports bribery and honest services fraud.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: A lawyer for Rozier, Jim Trusty, said: “The new indictment confirms that our motion to dismiss was a good one — it’s just new charges and new theories trotted out in the hope that something sticks.” Rozier has been charged with sports bribery and honest services fraud.

items.title: May 28, 1995: San Antonio’s Dennis Rodman breaks a camera lens in frustration during Game 4 of the Western Conference Finals in Houston.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
May 28, 1995: San Antonio’s Dennis Rodman breaks a camera lens in frustration during Game 4 of the Western Conference Finals in Houston.

Rodman had 12 PTS/19 REB as the Spurs evened up the series at 2-2 with a 103-81 win over the Rockets.
